exchange_a.name: Hyperliquid
strengths: Fully on-chain order book with CEX-grade performance
weaknesses: Newer platform with limited operational history

exchange_b.name: Aevo
strengths: Combined options and perpetuals trading platform
weaknesses: Lower overall liquidity compared to major DEXs

rate_mechanics: Both Hyperliquid and Aevo use standard 1-hour funding intervals with similar fee structures. Hyperliquid's fully on-chain order book provides transparent rate calculations directly on its L1, while Aevo uses off-chain order matching with on-chain settlement on its custom rollup. The key difference lies in Hyperliquid's real-time on-chain execution versus Aevo's hybrid approach combining options and perpetuals markets.
verdict: Hyperliquid offers superior transparency and decentralization with its fully on-chain order book, making it ideal for funding rate arbitrageurs who value on-chain verification. Aevo provides a more complete derivatives trading experience but with potentially less predictable funding mechanics due to its multi-product complexity.

faq.question: Which exchange offers better funding rate transparency?
faq.answer: Hyperliquid provides superior transparency with its fully on-chain order book where all funding rate calculations are verifiable on-chain. Aevo's hybrid model offers less direct visibility into rate mechanics.

faq.question: How do settlement speeds compare for funding arbitrage?
faq.answer: Hyperliquid offers sub-second finality on its native L1, while Aevo settles on its custom rollup with faster speeds than Ethereum mainnet but potentially higher latency than Hyperliquid's direct L1 execution.

faq.question: Which platform has better liquidity for large funding trades?
faq.answer: Hyperliquid generally offers deeper liquidity for perpetual futures, while Aevo's liquidity is more fragmented across its options and perpetuals products, potentially impacting large arbitrage positions.

faq.question: Are there collateral differences affecting funding strategies?
faq.answer: Hyperliquid only supports USDC collateral, limiting funding strategies to single-asset approaches. Aevo may offer more collateral options but with added complexity in cross-margin calculations.

seo.title: Hyperliquid vs Aevo: DEX Perpetual Futures Comparison
seo.description: Compare Hyperliquid and Aevo for perpetual futures trading. Analysis of funding rates, fees, liquidity, and on-chain mechanics for crypto derivatives arbitrage.
